# /spec

> Author the feature spec for a d365-fo feature. F&O specs are **feature-scoped** (per [ADR-0006](../../../design/adr/0006-doc-scope-domain-vs-feature.md)) — each feature lives in its own folder with spec/plan/fdd/tdd/blueprint/test-plan/tasks/output/reviews.

## Usage

```
/spec --feature <slug> [--source fresh|alm] [--project <name>]
```

`--source fresh` (default) drafts from RFP / user story / FastTrack process map. `--source alm` pulls work-items from ADO/JIRA via the `alm` agent.

## Inputs

- `agents.yaml`, `project.config.yaml`
- [templates/spec.template.md](../../templates/spec.template.md)
- Source material (RFP / user story / ALM work items)
- Existing F&O extension catalogue in `constitution/04-extension-coding-standards.md` (32-type catalogue)

## Execution flow

1. Validate `--feature` slug.
2. Resolve project; ensure `projects/{p}/d365-fo/features/{f}/` exists (or scaffold via `New-Feature.ps1`).
3. Read `project.config.yaml` for F&O-specific config (deployable-package strategy, INT object Key Vault, batch concurrency targets).
4. Render `spec.template.md` into `projects/{p}/d365-fo/features/{f}/spec.md` with sections:
   - §1 Business context
   - §2 Personas (typical: ERP user / batch operator / data steward / system admin)
   - §3 Functional requirements (FR-NN with ACs)
   - §4 NFR targets (per [constitution/08-nfr-targets.md](../../constitution/08-nfr-targets.md))
   - §5 Object-type scope (which of the 32-type catalogue applies; use object-id prefixes EXT/BDC/OPR/INT/DEN/SEC/WFL)
   - §6 Security keys / duties / privileges deltas
   - §7 Out of scope
5. Update `.workflow.json`: `phase=DEFINE`, `currentStates=[SPEC_DRAFT]`.

## Output

- `projects/{p}/d365-fo/features/{f}/spec.md`
- `.workflow.json` updated

## F&O-specific conventions

Per [constitution/06-documentation-and-change.md](../../constitution/06-documentation-and-change.md):
- ★ mandatory section markers — gate downstream commands
- "Not Applicable" explicit — never blank
- `<TBD>` convention — open items roll up into §1.5
